title: Avoid Plagiarism
summary: Use your own words and proper citation to avoid copying someone else's work.
learnerPromise: You will be able to use information from sources in your own work while giving proper credit and avoiding copying.
whyItMatters: Plagiarism means using someone else's words or ideas without credit, which is dishonest and can lead to serious consequences at school. Learning to use sources honestly is a key skill for all writing.
realLifeUses: Writing a research paper using facts from books and websites in your own words
Quoting a definition in an essay and giving proper credit
Creating a presentation that includes information from multiple sources
Paraphrasing a news article for a current events assignment
materialsNeeded: A short article or passage to practice with
result: A short paragraph that uses source information in your own words with proper credit
youWillLearn: How to tell the difference between quoting, paraphrasing, and copying
How to restate an idea in your own words while keeping the meaning
When and how to use quotation marks for exact wording
How to credit a source every time you use its information
miniChallenge: Read a short paragraph from an article. Write one sentence quoting it with quotation marks, and one sentence paraphrasing it in your own words. Add a simple citation to each.
steps: Read the original text and make sure you understand the main idea.
To quote: copy the exact words inside quotation marks and credit the source.
To paraphrase: cover the text and write the idea in your own words without looking.
Compare your version to the original to make sure you changed enough words.
Add a citation to every sentence that uses someone else's information.
completionCriteria: You can explain the difference between quoting and copying without credit
Your paraphrase uses mostly your own words and keeps the original meaning
Every use of source information includes a citation
commonProblems: Changing only a few words and thinking that counts as paraphrasing
Forgetting to cite a source because you did not use quotation marks
Copying the structure of the original sentence even when changing some words
tips: When paraphrasing, change both the words and the sentence structure
If you are not sure whether something needs a citation, include one just in case
Reading the source, covering it, and then writing helps you use your own words
